title: Sexual assault privacy and medical route
summary: Focused extreme medical support for sexual assault privacy and medical route in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: In extreme situations, survivors need safety, consent, privacy, evidence-aware choices, and urgent medical/psychological support without pressure. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”.
paper and pen. Context: “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Sexual assault privacy and medical route»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Sexual assault privacy and medical route»
steps: Move the survivor to safety if they want and it is safe.
Do not force them to talk, wash, report, or be examined.
Offer trusted support person, privacy, clean clothing, water, and transport options.
Seek professional care for injuries, pregnancy/STI concerns, forensic options, and mental support.
Record only what the survivor consents to record.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”.

Escalate immediately for trouble breathing, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, severe pain, chest pain, stroke signs, severe burns, pregnancy danger signs, infants in distress, poisoning, or rapid worsening. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”.
Do not delay evacuation or professional care to finish a checklist. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”.
Keep privacy, consent, dignity, and local law in mind even during collapse. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”.
common_mistakes: Trying to diagnose instead of recognizing danger signs while carrying out “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
Forgetting times, medicines, allergies, and changes while carrying out “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
Touching fluids or wounds without hand hygiene while carrying out “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
Moving an injured person without a safety reason while carrying out “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
Letting panic, crowds, or rumors control care while carrying out “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
alternatives: Use certified first-aid training where available as a fallback for “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
Use official emergency services or the nearest clinic as a fallback for “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
Use a pharmacist for medicine questions as a fallback for “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
Use radio/phone consultation if transport is delayed as a fallback for “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
Use written handover notes when devices fail as a fallback for “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
failure_signs: Breathing, bleeding, consciousness, pain, fever, confusion, weakness, or skin color gets worse during “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
The helper cannot keep the scene clean or safe during “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
Transport, water, power, or communication failure blocks care during “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
The person becomes unable to drink, speak, walk, recognize people, or stay awake during “Sexual assault privacy and medical route”
when_not_to_use: Do not use it to replace a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, poison center, or trained first-aider when using the “Sexual assault privacy and medical route” record
Do not use it for surgery or invasive treatment when using the “Sexual assault privacy and medical route” record
Do not use it to change prescription medicines or doses when using the “Sexual assault privacy and medical route” record
Do not force care on a conscious adult except immediate life-safety actions allowed by local rules when using the “Sexual assault privacy and medical route” record
short_term: Reduce immediate danger, call or send for qualified help, keep the person clean, warm or cool as needed, and record facts without guessing. This note applies specifically to Sexual assault privacy and medical route.
long_term: Build certified first-aid capacity, update medical records, protect supplies, practice handovers, and map routes to pharmacies, clinics, hospitals, and shelters. This note applies specifically to Sexual assault privacy and medical route.
if_method_fails: Escalate by the fastest safe route: emergency number, clinic, hospital, pharmacist, poison center, radio medical consult, trained first-aider, or evacuation convoy. Record what failed and when. This note applies specifically to Sexual assault privacy and medical route.
environment_notes: In apartments, shelters, rubble zones, heatwaves, floods, smoke, and outages, medical problems worsen because of crowding, poor air, poor hygiene, no elevator, no refrigeration, and delayed transport. This note applies specifically to Sexual assault privacy and medical route.
